ownergroup.description: Owner group of the service request. This group has overall responsibility for the service request. You can enter a value either in this field or the Owner field. Use the Select Action menu to assign an owner group. Note: this action may not be available to all users.

sitevisit.description: Falg used to indicate of a Ticket requires an on-site visit for investigation

calcshift.description: Along with the calculation calendar, sets the business hours that calculate the Target Contact, Target Response, and Target Resolution dates on a ticket or work order. Click the Select Value button to choose a shift.

actualcontactdate.description: Actual contact date. Enter a date or click the Select Date button and select a date.

reportedbyname.description: Name of person making the service request. Enter a value or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value.

impact.description: The Business Impact/severity of the Ticket
impact.title: Impact

pmcomresolution.description: Column identifies a ticket as a common request
pmcomresolution.title: Resolution

status.description: Status of the service request. If you are not viewing the Change Status dialog box, click the Change Status button in the toolbar to change the status. Note: this action may not be available to all users. Default statuses are NEW, QUEUED, PENDING, INPROG, RESOLVED, and CLOSED. See the online Help for more information on statuses.
status.title: Status

commodity.description: Service provided or purchased. Enter a value or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value.

hasld.description: Boolean flag to indicate if there is any long description for this record
hasld.title: Has Long Description

haschildren.description: Does this node have child nodes?
haschildren.title: Has Children

genassetdesc.description: Generate asset description when using this class structure?

reportedby.description: Identifies person making the service request. Enter a value or click the Select Value button.  (custom - added defaultvalue=personid)
reportedby.title: Reported By

assignedownergroup.description: Assigned Owner Group of the ticket record. This group has overall responsibility for the solution. Use the Select Action menu to assign an owner group. You can enter a value either in this field or the Owner field.

autolocate.description: String representation of the record geometry
autolocate.title: Formatted Geometry

origrecorgid.description: Organization Identifier of the originating Record
origrecorgid.title: Originating Record Oganization

vendor.description: Identifies the vendor for the service, service group, or asset. Enter a value or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value.
vendor.title: Vendor

assetnum.description: Identifies the primary asset on the service request. If the service request is for an asset, enter its identifier in the Asset field or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value. The Select Value dialog box, by default, displays all assets where the person ID in the Affected Person field is associated with an asset as either an asset user or an asset custodian. If the Asset Site field contains a value before you use the Select Value dialog box, the list of assets is always filtered by that value. After you select an asset, Maximo copies the asset location to the Location field. You can enter related assets and/or locations in the Related Assets table window. NOTE: These actions may not be available to all users.
assetnum.title: Asset

hasld.description: Boolean flag to indicate if there is any long description for this record
hasld.title: Has Long Description

iscrewworkgroup.description: Specifies whether the work group can be used in Crew applications. If the check box is  selected, the resource can be used. If the check box is cleared (the default), the resource cannot be used.
iscrewworkgroup.title: Crew Work Group

persongroup.description: Identifier of the person group record.
persongroup.title: Person Group

description.description: Description of the person group record.
description.title: Description

assetsiteid.description: Site of the asset on the service request. The default value is the site of the person record in the Affected User field. Enter a value or click the Select Value button. If the Asset Site field contains a value before you access the Select Value dialog box on the Asset or Location field, the list of assets or locations is always filtered by this value. You cannot edit this field after you have associated assets or locations with a record; you must remove the associations before you can edit this field.
assetsiteid.title: Asset Site

description.description: Describes the ticket template. To enter or view additional information, click the Long Description button.
description.title: Description

externalrefid.description: 'Identifies the ticket template. Hold Internal ID for source MAXIMO
externalrefid.title: External Ref ID

internalpriority.description: Importance of the ticket relative to other tickets as categorized by Service Desk personnel. Predefined values include: 1 (Urgent), 2 (High), 3 (Medium), and 4 (Low). Click the Select Value button to choose an internal priority.
internalpriority.title: Internal Priority

ownergroup.description: Functional group or organization that owns this type of ticket. Click the Detail Menu button to select a person group or go to the Person Groups application to create one.
ownergroup.title: Owner Group

assignedownergroup.description: Assigned Owner Group of the ticket template record. Use the Select Action menu to assign an owner group.
assignedownergroup.title: Assigned Owner Group

vendor.description: If ticket requires work to be performed by an outside company, name of vendor responsible for the work. Click the Detail Menu button to select a value or go to the Companies application to create one.
vendor.title: Vendor

owner.description: Individual person responsible for the ticket. Click the Detail Menu button to select a person or go to the People application to create one.
owner.title: Owner

templateid.description: Identifies the ticket template. This value must be unique for all ticket template records.
templateid.title: Template

orgid.description: Organization associated with the ticket. Click the Select Value button choose an organization.
orgid.title: Organization

hasld.description: Boolean flag to indicate if there is any long description for this record
hasld.title: Has Long Description

status.description: Status of the ticket template. When you create a template, Maximo automatically sets the status to DRAFT. When you are ready for Maximo users to apply the template, set the status to ACTIVE. Set the status of a template to INACTIVE if you do not want it to be used.
status.title: Status

relatedtoglobal.description: Flag used to determine if a Ticket is related to a global issue Ticket
relatedtoglobal.title: Related to Global Issue

relatedrecclass.description: Class of the related ticket. Maximo enters this value for the related ticket you select. You can choose a ticket class before choosing a related ticket; if you do so, Maximo displays only tickets of the chosen class in the Select Value dialog box for the related ticket. To choose a ticket class, enter a value or click the Select Value button.
relatedrecclass.title: Class

relatetype.description: Describes the relationship of this record to the record in the key field.
relatetype.title: Relationship

relatedreckey.description: Identifies the related ticket. Enter a value or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value.
relatedreckey.title: Related Record Key

failurecode.description: Indicates Top level of a Failure Hierarchy
failurecode.title: Failure Code

location.description: Identifies the ticket's location. This is not necessarily the asset's location, however, if an asset is entered, its location will default here. If the service request is for a location, enter its identifier in the Location field or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value. The Select Value dialog box, by default, displays all locations where the person ID in the Affected Person field is associated with a location record either as location user or as a location custodian. If the Asset Site field contains a value before you use the Select Value dialog box, the list of locations is always filtered by that value. After you select a location, Maximo copies it to the Location field. If the selected location has only a single asset associated with it, Maximo enters its identifier in the Asset field. You can enter related assets and/or locations in the Related Assets table window. NOTE: These options may not be available to all users.
location.title: Location

isglobal.description: Flag used to determine if a Ticket is a Global Issue
isglobal.title: Is Global Issue

If the check box is cleared (the default), the solution record is unavailable to self-service users. In the More Search Fields dialog box, you must specify a Y or N in this field.
selfservaccess.title: Self-Service Access

changedate.description: This is when the solution was last modified
changedate.title: Changed Date

failurecode.title: Failure Code

fr1code_longdescription.title: Cause

status.description: Status, or state, of the solution record. Predefined statuses include DRAFT, ACTIVE, and INACTIVE. Only solution records that are set to ACTIVE can be viewed or found in a search outside of this administrative Solutions application. Solutions records in DRAFT or INACTIVE statuses are managed within this Solutions application.
status.title: Status

assetnum.description: Identifies the primary asset on the incident record. If the incident record is for an asset, enter its identifier in the Asset field or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value. The Select Value dialog box, by default, displays all assets where the person ID in the Affected Person field is associated with an asset as either an asset user or an asset custodian. If the Asset Site field contains a value before you use the Select Value dialog box, the list of assets is always filtered by that value. After you select an asset, Maximo copies the asset location to the Location field. You can enter related assets and/or locations in the Related Assets table window.

assetsiteid.description: Site of the asset on the incident record. The default value is the site of the person record in the Affected User field. Enter a value or click the Select Value button. If the Asset Site field contains a value before you access the Select Value dialog box on the Asset or Location field, the list of assets or locations is always filtered by this value. You cannot edit this field after you have associated assets or locations with a record; you must remove the associations before you can edit this field.

If the check box is selected, the self-service user can view this record. If the check box is cleared the solution record is not visible to the self-service user.

urgency.title: Urgency

reportedphone.title: Phone

owner.description: Owner of the incident record. This person has overall responsibility for the incident. Use the Select Action menu to assign an owner. You can enter a value either in this field or the Owner Group field.
owner.title: Owner

calcorgid.description: Organization associated with the calculation calendar, and used with the Calendar and Shift fields to calculate the Target Contact, Target Response, and Target Resolution dates on a ticket or work order. Click the Detail Menu button to select an organization or go to the Organizations application to create one.
calcorgid.title: Organization

pmcomtype.description: Column identifies a ticket as a common request
pmcomtype.title: Process Manager Type

affectedemail.description: E-mail address of person affected by the incident.
affectedemail.title: E-mail

fr2code_longdescription.description: Long description of the resolution. If you entered a value in the Solution field, Maximo may enter data here, but you can edit it.
fr2code_longdescription.title: Resolution

relatedtoglobal.description: Flag used to determine if a Ticket is related to a global issue Ticket
relatedtoglobal.title: Related to Global Issue

targetfinish.description: Target finish date. Enter a date or click the Select Date button.
targetfinish.title: Target Finish

failurecode.title: Failure Class

fr1code_longdescription.description: Long description of the cause. If you entered a value in the Solution field, Maximo may enter data here, but you can edit it.
fr1code_longdescription.title: Cause

location.description: Identifies the primary location on the incident record. If the incident record is for a location, enter its identifier in the Location field or click the Detail Menu button to select an option and retrieve a value. The Select Value dialog box, by default, displays all locations where the person ID in the Affected Person field is associated with a location record either as location user or as a location custodian. If the Asset Site field contains a value before you use the Select Value dialog box, the list of locations is always filtered by that value. After you select a location, Maximo copies it to the Location field. If the selected location has only a single asset associated with it, Maximo enters its identifier in the Asset field. You can enter related assets and/or locations in the Related Assets table window.
location.title: Location

isglobal.description: Specifies whether the ticket is a global one. A global ticket either affects many users or is a root cause of other tickets. If the check box is selected, the ticket is a global. You can view or specify its class in the Global Class field. If the check box is cleared, the ticket is not global.
isglobal.title: Global Issue

401.description:
BMXAA0033I Your session timed out due to inactivity.
BMXAA0026E The action could not be performed because the application server security is enabled. Proper authorization is required to perform this action. Report this error to your system administrator.
BMXAA7901E You cannot log in at this time. Contact the system administrator.
BMXAA0025E The method cannot be called because application server security is not enabled. Enable Application Server Security. For details, see information on defining server security in the information center.
BMXAA4122E You cannot be connected to an application server on the specified host. Ensure that an application server is running.
BMXAA0020E The session that is used to access business objects is either not valid or has timed out. Verify that the remote system is avaialble, then log in and authenticate again.
BMXAA0021E User name and password combination are not valid. Try again.
BMXAA0011E User {0} is inactive. Specify a different user.

412.description: BMXAA8229W Record {0} has been updated by another user. Your changes have not been saved. Refresh the record and try again.

413.description: BMXAA2249E The file being attached exceeds the size limit of {0}MB. See your system administrator to have the limit adjusted.

403.description:
BMXAA1671I Skip transaction.
BMXAA2283E Your password has expired.

404.description:
BMXAA4216E Unknown Object named {0} has been encountered. Gather the logs from the directory and determine the cause and source of the error.
BMXAA9510E The stable page resource was not found because the reload of a stable page was attempted or the idle expiry time has expired.
BMXAA4187E The relationship {0} does not exist for business object {1}. Verify that the relationship specified in parameter {0} exists in the Maxrelationship table.
BMXAA1283E The {0} value in the Consumed By field cannot be used. Define an object structure with a Consumed By value that is valid for the processing action.
BMXAA8727E The OSLC resource {0} with the ID {1} was not found as it does not exist in the system. In the database, verify whether the resource for the ID exists.

415.description: BMXAA8823E The format {0} is not supported. You must use a supported format such as application/json.

405.description: BMXAA8815E The HTTP method DELETE is not permitted on a collection resource. Only HTTP GET and POST methods are supported on collection URIs.

409.description: BMXAA9524E The transaction ID {1} already exists in the OSLC transaction table with resource ID {2} for object structure {3}.

opmodeor.name: opmodeor
opmodeor.description: if set to 1, this will cause the oslc.where clause to set the query mode to OR clause instead of AND.

oslc.properties.name: oslc.properties
oslc.properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource (like an individual workorder).

transactionid.name: transactionid
transactionid.description: This is used to prevent de-duplication for create/update/delete. If this header is present then the value is compared to a list of committed transaction ids.If it has a match, then it is considered as a duplicate transaction and an HTTP conflict error is generated.

oslc.where.name: oslc.where
oslc.where.description: Where clause to filter the set of resources

x-allow-events.name: x-allow-events
x-allow-events.description: For a create/update/delete, if the request wants to suppress the object events, this request header needs to be set to 0. By default all events are fired unless programmatically stopped.

x-method-override.name: x-method-override
x-method-override.description: Due to constraints from browser the HTTP POST method is re-used for creates and updates and deletes.This request header is used to convey the specifics of the POST request. PATCH is used for update. DELETE is used for deleting the resource. BULK is used for supporting bulk operations.If this header is not present, the POST request is treated as a create request.

gbrange.name: gbrange
gbrange.description: The range clause for the group by query.

lean.name: lean
lean.description: If set to 1, this will instruct the api to work with the "lean" json format as opposed to the namespaced json that it produces by default. This option is supported only starting 7601 release of the Maximo base. Its preferred to use this format as it reduces the size of the json payload.

oslc.select.name: oslc.select
oslc.select.description:
Filter clause following the OSLC query syntax
(see below for more details on this)

gbcols.name: gbcols
gbcols.description: The comma separated group by columns.

content-type.name: content-type
content-type.description: The mime-type of the request document.

gbsortby.name: gbsortby
gbsortby.description: The order by clause for the group by query. Follows the same syntax as oslc.orderBy.

id.name: id
id.description: Unique id for object structure resource

collectioncount.name: collectioncount
collectioncount.description: If set to 1, it will instruct the api to include the total count of records that matched the query ( responseInfo.totalCount). By default it will not return that unless the maximo property mxe.oslc.collectioncount is set to 1. This is a recommended way to control collection count at the request level.

oslc.searchTerms.name: oslc.searchTerms
oslc.searchTerms.description: Record wide text search for tokens specified as this query parameter value. Example oslc.searchTerms="pump","broken"

oslc.paging.name: oslc.paging
oslc.paging.description: Instruct the server to page the query response based on the default page size. The default page size needs to be configured on the server side for this to take effect.

apikey.name: apikey
apikey.description: Set to the users apikey. This is used when the 'api' context is used (instead of 'oslc'). Do make sure that the apikey is generated for the user. You can use the Administration app to generate an apikey

oslc.pageSize.name: oslc.pageSize
oslc.pageSize.description: Instruct the server to page the query result with the specified page size.

stablepaging.name: stablepaging
stablepaging.description: If set to true, will instruct the server to page the resource from a in session MboSet.

accept.name: accept
accept.description: The mime-type of the accept document.

patchtype.name: patchtype
patchtype.description: This request header with the value of MERGE will instruct the server to treat a PATCH request (POST with x-method-override as PATCH) processor to merge the child objects (array of child objects) in the request with the corresponding child elements in the server. If this header is not present, the server will delete the child objects that are missing from the request.

properties.name: properties
properties.description: Select clause for selecting a partial view of the member resource.
